questions.question: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort?
questions.explanation: Selection Sort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm that works by selecting the minimum element from the unsorted part of the list and swapping it with the first element of the unsorted part. It has a time complexity of O(n^2) because it makes n comparisons in each iteration of the algorithm.

questions.question: How does Bubble Sort work?
options: It repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order. The pass through the list is repeated until the list is sorted.
It starts with the last element and moves one element at a time towards the first element.
It compares the first two elements and if they are in the correct order, it moves to the next two elements.
It swaps the first two elements and then moves to the next two elements until the end of the list is reached.
questions.explanation: Bubble Sort is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order. The pass through the list is repeated until the list is sorted.

questions.question: What is the advantage of Selection Sort over Bubble Sort?
options: It is faster for large lists.
It can handle lists of floating point numbers.
questions.explanation: One advantage of Selection Sort over Bubble Sort is that it is simpler to implement. Selection Sort only needs to keep track of the minimum element, whereas Bubble Sort needs to keep track of the maximum element as well.

questions.question: What is the disadvantage of Bubble Sort?
options: It is faster for large lists.
It can handle lists of floating point numbers.
It is not suitable for lists with duplicate elements.
questions.explanation: One disadvantage of Bubble Sort is that it is not suitable for lists with duplicate elements, as it can get stuck in an infinite loop if the list contains elements that are equal to the first element.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort?
questions.explanation: Bubble Sort has a time complexity of O(n^2) because it makes n-1 comparisons in each iteration of the algorithm, and there are n-1 iterations in total.
